$comment: Complete style map for the Reusable Site Template. Purpose: let an agent author a new, drop-in-replaceable theme. Read meta.how_to_create_a_theme first, then `tokens` (the swappable vocabulary), then `elements` (what each token paints) and `stage` (the animated Home hero, which has theme-specific literals to override). Generated 2026-06-26 against the live CSS.

meta.styling_model: CSS design tokens. No raw color/font literals in markup. Brand-level appearance flows through CSS custom properties (:root) that a theme overrides; only intentionally-decorative art (planets, starfield, nebula) is hardcoded.

files.token_emitter: resources/views/partials/tokens.blade.php  (renders <style>:root{ --token: value }</style> in <head>; merges config('tokens.defaults') with SiteSetting.branding)
files.branding_store: SiteSetting.branding (DB JSON column; the active theme's token overrides)
files.stage_js: public/js/solarsystem.js (twinkle-star generation + mouse parallax; self-guards on .stage so it is inert on non-Home pages)

activation.apply_theme: php artisan app:apply-theme {name}   (writes config('themes.{name}') into SiteSetting.branding)
activation.reset: php artisan app:apply-theme default          (clears branding → back to config/tokens.php light defaults)

how_to_create_a_theme: 1. PALETTE/FONTS ONLY (recolor, no new hero art): add a new array to config/themes.php with values for the tokens listed in `tokens`. Add @font-face rules to public/css/fonts.css for any new self-hosted fonts and reference the family names in the font-* tokens. Run `php artisan app:apply-theme {yourname}`. This re-skins every page EXCEPT the hardcoded parts of the animated stage (see `stage.theme_specific_literals`).

2. NEW HERO LOOK: the .stage hero (public/css/hero-solarsystem.css) is purpose-built cosmic art (orbiting planets, starfield). For a non-cosmic theme, either (a) tokenize/override the literals in `stage.theme_specific_literals`, or (b) replace resources/views/partials/hero.blade.php + hero-solarsystem.css with your own hero and drop solarsystem.js. Keep hero copy bound to SiteSetting.hero (no hardcoded text).
3. To make the stage FULLY token-swappable, replace the literals in `stage.theme_specific_literals` with their `suggested_token` so a palette swap also recolors the hero chrome.
4. Keep the structure-vs-skin split: positional CSS in structure.css, all visual CSS token-driven in skin.css.

5. Preserve the test contract: skin.css must keep the strings `.hero`, `var(--hero-overlay)`, `var(--font-display)`, `var(--color-heading)`, and the CKEditor `.image-style-*` rules; config/tokens.php defaults must stay light (e.g. --nav-height: 4rem).
principles: Swap a theme by overriding tokens, not by editing markup.
Inner pages (About/Contact/Blog) are 100% token-driven — they re-skin with zero CSS edits.
The animated Home stage is partly tokenized and partly hardcoded cosmic art — see `stage`.

UI chrome is non-selectable: nav, .brand, h1-h3, and all .stage hero text use user-select:none with cursor:default (no text caret/highlight); only links & buttons show the pointer. Article/body and .muted text stay selectable. Defined in skin.css and hero-solarsystem.css.

token_types.color: any CSS color; may be consumed directly or via color-mix(in srgb, var(--token) N%, transparent)
token_types.font-stack: a CSS font-family list; the first family must have @font-face entries in fonts.css
token_types.length: a CSS length; some are multiplied via calc(var(--token) * n)

color-primary.role: Primary brand / accent: links, primary button fill/border, eyebrow text, ghost-button text, scroll-cue arrow.

color-accent.role: Secondary accent / hover-highlight: link hover, nav link hover & active, button hover text.

color-bg.role: Page background; also the .stage base background and the source for the translucent nav (via color-mix). Legacy hero-cta text color.

color-bg-alt.role: Elevated surfaces: card/panel background, nav bottom border.

color-fg.role: Body / default text. Also card border tint (via color-mix) and the stage lede text.

color-heading.role: Headings (h1–h3), brand wordmark, stage hero title.

color-muted.role: Secondary / muted text: .muted, inactive nav links, figcaption, legacy hero subhead, stage nav links (idle).

font-heading.role: Headings (h1–h3) on inner pages; conceptually the stage hero title (currently hardcoded there — see stage literals).

consumed_by: .brand / h1,h2,h3,.brand {font-family} @ skin.css (line 23; note the later Solar System h1,h2,h3 rule overrides heading font with font-heading, leaving .brand on font-display)

space-unit.role: Spacing scale base, multiplied via calc(var(--space-unit) * n) for padding/margins/gaps.

radius.role: Corner radius for cards, content images, legacy hero CTA.

container-width.role: Max width of centered content + nav.

hero-overlay.role: Dark scrim over the LEGACY .hero starfield (not the .stage). Retained for the mystik hero + test contract.

selectors: body @ skin.css (bg, color, font, static CSS starfield)
literals: 6-layer radial-gradient starfield (white/icy dots) with background-attachment: fixed — decorative; visible only on a dark color-bg
elements.notes: On a light theme the starfield dots are invisible (white-on-light); harmless. Remove/adjust the body background-image block for a non-starry theme.

selectors: h1,h2,h3 @ skin.css line 67 (Solar System block wins)
elements.notes: An earlier rule (line 23) sets headings+.brand to font-display; the later Solar System rule re-sets h1–h3 to font-heading. Net: headings=font-heading, .brand=font-display.
On the Home stage the brand is styled by `.stage .brand` with HARDCODED font/color — see stage literals.

elements.applies_to: all pages (single partial; transparent overlay on Home via .page-home)
selectors: nav @ skin.css (translucent bg via color-mix, blur, border)
.page-home nav @ skin.css (transparent, no border on Home)
elements.notes: Inner pages: sticky translucent bar. Home: transparent overlay over the stage.
These ARE fully token-driven. The visually-identical buttons inside the .stage are NOT (hardcoded) — see stage literals.
Two .card rules exist (later wins for border opacity); behavior correct, could be consolidated.
Layout/alignment for rich-text images. Class names are produced by CKEditor; do not rename. Required by SkinCssTest.

elements.applies_to: NOT used by the Solar System markup; belongs to the mystik/CSS-starfield hero. Retained for mystik + test contract.
elements.notes: The current Home hero uses `.stage` markup (see stage). These classes still render the CSS-starfield hero if a theme/markup uses them.

stage.description: The animated Solar System Home hero. Full-viewport (.stage), scoped CSS so it never leaks to inner pages. Markup: resources/views/partials/hero.blade.php (cosmos layers + 5 orbits/planets + sun + nebula + vignette + hero copy + scroll cue). Behavior: public/js/solarsystem.js.

theme_specific_literals.$comment: These hardcode the Solar System icy palette / fonts instead of tokens. A palette swap via app:apply-theme will NOT recolor them. To make the stage fully swappable, replace each `value` with its `suggested_token`.

decorative_art_literals.$comment: Purpose-built cosmic art — NOT brand tokens. For a non-cosmic theme, replace or remove these wholesale rather than tokenizing.

geometry_vars.--solar-scale: scales the whole solar-system cluster; set on .stage (1.18) and overridden in media queries (1.0 / 0.72 / 0.52)
geometry_vars.--px / --py: parallax offsets set by solarsystem.js on .solar-wrap
geometry_vars.--dur / --delay: per-star twinkle timing set by solarsystem.js

fonts.used_for: font-heading (Solar System headings + stage title literal)

known_inconsistencies_for_theme_author: Stage buttons/brand/title hardcode the icy palette & fonts (see stage.theme_specific_literals) — a token-only swap recolors inner pages but not those stage elements. Tokenize them for a fully swappable theme.
skin.css has duplicate body / h1,h2,h3 / .card rules (original light-theme block + appended Solar System block; the later wins). Cosmetic; consolidate if rewriting.
Legacy .hero* classes (CSS-starfield hero) coexist with the .stage hero; only .stage is used by current Home markup.
